kill_fact: No kill_fact breaks the bear thesis — the adversarial attack CONFIRMS it. Every load-bearing bear fact verifies against primary/credible sources: (1) Q2 FY26 revenue $23.9B / non-GAAP GM ~75% / non-GAAP EPS $12.20 are a CYCLICAL+AI peak, not a steady state (FY25 full-year non-GAAP GM ~39.8%, FY23 loss-making); (2) the HBM4 Vera Rubin catalyst FIRED June 1-2 2026 and fired AGAINST Micron — Nvidia certified ALL THREE suppliers (SK Hynix 60-70% / Samsung 25-30% / Micron 'the remainder', the SMALLEST allocation), structurally margin-dilutive; (3) the stock is up ~297-300% YTD, hit an ATH $1,149.43 on June 18 2026, and trades ABOVE the average analyst target (~$945.6, 44 analysts). The AI-scale-out demand is already PRICED IN, the catalyst is spent and out-of-window, and the entire residual upside is a levered bet on the single shared basket factor (AI-capex durability). This is a quality name at an inverted risk/reward — exactly the C/PASS the debate reached.
corrections: (1) DATE: dossier implies Q2 FY26 was reported in February 2026; the print was actually March 18, 2026 (quarter ended Feb 26, 2026). Immaterial to the thesis but the 'sop built on stale pre-print financials' attack FAILS — financials are current. (2) NET-CASH UNVERIFIED: the ~$5.8B net-cash claim (cash ~$13.9B vs debt ~$10.1B) could not be confirmed against the SEC press release (sec.gov returned HTTP 403); it is directionally credible and NOT load-bearing for the bear (the bear case rests on valuation/positioning, not solvency), but the precise figure should be re-checked against the 10-Q balance sheet before being cited as fact.
evidence: SEC Form 8-K (Ex-99.1 press release), Micron Technology, fiscal Q2 2026 (quarter ended Feb 26 2026), filed ~Mar 18 2026: revenue $23.9B +196% YoY, GAAP gross margin 74.4% (vs 36.8% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $12.20; Q3 FY26 guide $33.5B +/- $750M, GM ~81%, non-GAAP EPS $19.15 +/- $0.40 — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000723125/000072312526000004/

StreetInsider / Yahoo Finance / TechTimes, June 1-2 2026 (GTC Taipei): Nvidia certified Samsung, SK Hynix AND Micron for Vera Rubin HBM4 full production (Q3 2026 ship); supply-chain estimates SK Hynix ~60-70%, Samsung ~25-30%, Micron 'the remainder' (smallest allocation)
